nameMeanings.name: Jenkin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal names Jankin, Jenkin, and Jonkin, pet forms of Jan, Jen, and Jon + the hypocoristic suffix -kin. In Middle English, names in -kin were often used to name the younger of two family members with the same personal name.

variants.name: Jenkins
meanings: English and Welsh: variant of Jenkin , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. Jenkins is one of the most common surnames in England, especially southwestern England, but is also especially associated with Wales.

variants.name: Jenks
meanings: English (West Midlands): from the Middle English personal name Jenke (a shortening of the personal name Jenkin, Jankin; see Jenkin ), with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

History: Joseph Jenks (1602–83), the descendant of an old Welsh family, was born in England and traveled to Saugus, near Lynn, MA, in 1642 to assist in the development of America's first iron works. His son, Joseph Jenckes (sic), followed in 1650, founded Pawtucket, RI, and raised four sons who held places of respect and distinction in RI, including one who served as governor for five years.

variants.name: Jenkinson
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): from the Middle English personal names Jankin, Jenkin, or Jonkin (see Jenkin ) + son.

nameMeanings.name: Hywel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a vocabulary word meaning ‘eminent, conspicuous’; it was common in the Middle Ages and was revived in the 20th-century.

variants.name: Hywell
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a vocabulary word meaning ‘eminent, conspicuous’; it was common in the Middle Ages and was revived in the 20th-century.

variants.name: Howell
meanings: Anglicized form of the Welsh name Hywel , or a transferred use of the surname derived from that name.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: Hywel ap Jenkin was born in Pembrokeshire, Wales as the son of Jenkin "Llwyd" ap Ieuan and Efa ferch Maredudd. He had at least 2 sons with Elen Banw.

fr.lifeSummary: Hywel ap Jenkin est né à Pembrokeshire, Galles, fils de Jenkin "Llwyd" ap Ieuan et Efa ferch Maredudd. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Elen Banw.

de.lifeSummary: Hywel ap Jenkin wurde in Pembrokeshire, Wales, als Sohn von Jenkin "Llwyd" ap Ieuan und Efa ferch Maredudd geboren. Er hatte mit Elen Banw mindestens 2 Söhne.

it.lifeSummary: Hywel ap Jenkin è nato il nel Pembrokeshire, Galles come figlio di Jenkin "Llwyd" ap Ieuan e Efa ferch Maredudd. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Elen Banw.

pt.lifeSummary: Hywel ap Jenkin nasceu em Pembrokeshire, Gales filho de Jenkin "Llwyd" ap Ieuan e Efa ferch Maredudd. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Elen Banw.

ru.lifeSummary: Hywel ap Jenkin родился в местности пемброкешир, Уэльс. Его родителей звали Jenkin "Llwyd" ap Ieuan и Efa ferch Maredudd. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Elen Banw.

es.lifeSummary: Hywel ap Jenkin nació en Pembrokeshire, Gales como hijo de Jenkin "Llwyd" ap Ieuan y Efa ferch Maredudd. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Elen Banw.
